calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-18-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-17-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Samuel P. Cheadle, esq. (415-381-5599) of Chavez & Gertler LLP for Plaintiff, Joan Dower, via Court Call.
Douglas E. Johns, esq. (650-592-5400) of Coddington Hicks & Danforth for Cross-Defendant, Tu Dinh.
Shahrad Milanfar, esq. (510-658-3600) of echerer Kannett & Schweitzer for Defendants, Open Top Sightseeing USA, et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT TU DINH's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Cross-Defendant Tu Quy Dinh's motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. Mr. Dinh provides substantial evidence that the amount of the settlement is within the reasonable range of his proportional share of comparative liability for the plaintiff's injuries, and Defendants Open Top Sightseeing USA, Open Top Sightseeing San Francisco San Francisco, LLC, and John Frederich Kennedy fail to show that the settlement is out of the ballpark. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d) [the party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue]; Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Mr. Dinh has agreed to pay $50,000 to Plaintiff Joan Dower, the entire amount of his available liability insurance coverage, and his sealed declaration, which has been provided to the Court and to Defendants, shows that he has exceedingly modest assets and is effectively judgment proof. (Johns Decl., Ex. 7; see Dole Food Co., Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 894, 909 [relevant considerations include the financial conditions and insurance policy limits of settling defendants]; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1149, 1152 ["Notwithstanding the settlement's disproportionality, because Guerrero lacks other assets, substantial evidence supports the finding of good faith settlement"].) Defendants' speculation that Mr. Dinh "likely" has substantial undisclosed assets is unsupported by any admissible evidence and is insufficient to meet their burden. (See Cahill v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 939, 968 [because party opposing motion "had the burden to prove the settlement was not made in good faith, it could have presented evidence on those issues to show Owners had the financial capacity or insurance coverage limits to pay a reasonable settlement amount"]; Brehm Communities v. Superior Court (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 730, 736 ["the determination whether the settlement was in good faith must be based on competent, admissible evidence"].) Defendants' belated request for discovery into Mr. Dinh's financial condition is denied.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To File Exhibit Under Seal
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Samuel P. Cheadle, esq. (415-381-5599) of Chavez & Gertler LLP for Plaintiff, Joan Dower, via Court Call.
Douglas E. Johns, esq. (650-592-5400) of Coddington Hicks & Danforth for Cross-Defendant, Tue Dinh.
Shahrad Milanfar, esq. (510-658-3600) of echerer Kannett & Schweitzer for Defendants, Open Top Sightseeing USA, et al.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT TU DINH's Motion To File Exhibit Under Seal.

Grant. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
